    ensures

{
}
reason: Writing to a persistent memory region should not change its length — if the map-based write accidentally altered the sequence length, all offset calculations would be invalidated

name: write_within_range_has_outstanding
code:
proof fn phi_5_write_within_range_has_outstanding(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView, addr: int, bytes: Seq<u8>, k: int)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        bytes.len() > 0,
        addr <= k < addr + bytes.len(),
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).state[k].outstanding_write == Some(bytes[k - addr]),
{
}
reason: After writing, positions within the range should have the new byte as an outstanding write — if `PersistentMemoryByte::write` didn't properly record the outstanding write, flush_byte would return stale data
